title: CGPA to Percentage Conversion for All Indian Universities 2026
summary: Each university has a different CGPA to percentage formula. Find the exact conversion for VTU, Anna University, Mumbai University, JNTU, DU and more.
content:
# CGPA to Percentage — All Indian Universities 2026

Different universities use different formulas. Using the wrong one gives an incorrect percentage on job applications.

## University-Wise Formulas

| University | Formula | Example (CGPA 8.5) |
|---|---|---|
| VTU (Karnataka) | (CGPA − 0.75) × 10 | 77.5% |
| Anna University (TN) | (CGPA × 10) − 7.5 | 77.5% |
| Mumbai University | CGPA × 9.5 | 80.75% |
| Delhi University | CGPA × 9.5 | 80.75% |
| JNTU (Telangana/AP) | CGPA × 10 | 85% |
| RGPV (MP) | (CGPA − 0.5) × 10 | 80% |
| Pune University (SPPU) | (CGPA × 10) − 7.5 | 77.5% |
| IP University (Delhi) | CGPA × 9.5 | 80.75% |
| Calicut University | (CGPA × 10) − 5 | 80% |

Use our [CGPA Calculator](/tools/cgpa-calculator.html) for automatic conversion.

## IIT/NIT — No Official Formula

Most IITs and NITs don't officially convert CGPA to percentage. Common approximation: Percentage ≈ CGPA × 9.5. Many companies accept CGPA directly for IIT/NIT graduates.

## For US/UK Applications (4.0 Scale)

- CGPA 9.0–10.0 → ~4.0 GPA
- CGPA 8.0–8.9 → ~3.5–3.9 GPA
- CGPA 7.0–7.9 → ~3.0–3.4 GPA
- CGPA 6.0–6.9 → ~2.5–2.9 GPA

## If Your University Isn't Listed

Default safe formula: **Percentage = CGPA × 9.5**. State which formula you used if asked.
